figures.caption: Figure 6. Supervised PIEFS: Gram error ∥C − I∥2F vs. test accuracy. All dataset–metric variants (circles = OFF, squares = DIAG, triangles = TROTTER) achieve high accuracy; orthogonality quality does not limit performance under supervision.

Figure 7. Unsupervised PIEFS: Gram error ∥C − I∥2F vs. test accuracy. Learnable-metric variants (squares, triangles) attain lower Gram error yet lower accuracy than OFF (circles), revealing the metric-degeneracy effect described in App. C.

Figure 2. Leading learned coordinate maps ϕ1, ϕ2, ϕ3 (spectral view). Each panel shows one coordinate map ϕj(x1, x2) as a heatmap; dots are validation points colored by class. Unlike Fig. 1, no decision-boundary contour is overlaid: these are the raw learned coordinates, showing the dominant mode ϕ1 (absent from Fig. 1) and the progression to higher modes. PIEFS-off, same representative replicate as in Fig. 1.
Table 1. Training components used in the reported experiments.

Figure 1. Classifier geometry induced by PIEFS coordinates. Columns 1–2 show ϕ2 and ϕ3 on Two Moons and Circles (PIEFS-off, one seed). Column 3 shows the signed classifier margin (linear-readout logit) pulled back to the input plane (x1, x2). Figure 2 shows the corresponding individual coordinate heatmaps for the same replicate.
